text: hi I'm Nicole Ellis I'm a certified
professional dog trainer my dog Maggie
and I go everywhere together in our
family we often take trips the Maggie
can come along and enjoy too
proper planning can take a lot of the
stress out of traveling with your pet
here's some of my top tips for traveling
with the dog step 1 plan out your trip
for road trips planned stops for water
and potty breaks it's for you both to
throw ball and take in the sights keep a
bag of items in the car for road trips
which include the following an extra
leash a collapsible water bowl extra
poop bags treats and a towel you never
know when adventures may get messy or
muddy having this bag with me allows me
to quickly grab what I need from Maggie
without searching through my luggage
step 2
pack your pets food or call stores in
the area to confirm you can find it
there the last thing you need on
vacation is to be dealing with an upset
doggy tummy due to switching food hmm
step 3
be prepared in case of an injury safety
on the road is huge for me
I've seen too many dogs that have become
injured or worse in a car accident
make sure your harness or carrier is
approved by the Center for pet safety
many companies tests their own equipment
but that's not good enough in a 30
mile-per-hour crash a 12 pound dog
smaller than Maggie exerts a thousand
pounds of force which is not only
dangerous for the driver but also the
passenger Maggie always rides in a
Sleepypod harness it allows her to sit
and lay down comfortably most car
accidents with pets inside due to
distractions caused by the pet a harness
or carrier will help prevent this if
you're flying be sure to call ahead of
time and speak to your airline and check
the rules as they fluctuate between
companies
step for plan dog friendly activities
when traveling check ahead of time which
activities are actually pet friendly and
also which your pet will enjoy man he
isn't a huge fan of the exams and she
probably preferred a state or Rover
sitters home and play fetch all day
knowing in advance allows you to reach
out to sitters in your area if your dog
can't come join along step 5
check what pet friendly hotel really
it's some hotels
this means your pet is welcome in the
room as you come and go other times this
means your dog is welcome as long as
you're in the room but they can't be
left on a company step 6
have a great time and create some
amazing memories together
[Music]
